behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the vision model’s real classification quality on explicit imagery; a stubbed classifier would always return expected labels and can hide true false negatives. I considered Workflow but rejected it because the behavior text focuses on a single model decision plus one hold/publish gate, not a multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run known-explicit image samples through the real moderation path with the real vision model enabled; assert each submission is marked held-for-review and no publish action is emitted.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the text model’s real robustness to adversarial obfuscation. Deterministic mocks cannot validate model judgment under these transformations. I considered Workflow but rejected it because behavior text describes one classification decision, not multiple agentic stages chained together.
behaviors.test_description: Use a curated adversarial corpus of hate-speech variants (leet substitutions, Unicode confusables, spacing tricks) against the real text moderation model and assert they are held/flagged.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: This behavior’s primary risk is orchestration/state handoff across local modules (image-result + text-result -> final publish decision), which is reproducible with deterministic model outputs. I considered Agentic but rejected it because the behavior text is about decision routing after both checks pass, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service, persistence, and publisher modules together; force both check results to SAFE via deterministic test doubles; assert final state is published, no hold record exists, and no drop path is taken.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires multiple agentic steps in sequence (vision classification + text classification) and end-to-end pipeline timing, which matches Workflow. I considered System but rejected it because the behavior text explicitly demands a full multi-step agentic pipeline, not only infrastructure timing.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions through the real pipeline with both real AI models enabled; measure submission-to-terminal-decision latency and assert completion within 5 seconds at target peak-hour load profile.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears at real external-infrastructure boundaries (model endpoint outage/timeout/error semantics) and retry queue interaction. In-process mocks can hide transport and timeout behaviors that trigger wrong fallback decisions. I considered Agentic but rejected it because behavior text is strictly about unavailability/fallback routing, not model-content judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real unavailability conditions for the vision endpoint (e.g., connection refusal/timeout/5xx via test environment) and submit image posts; assert they enter retry queue and are never auto-approved.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Borderline consistency depends on real model behavior under repeated evaluation; mocks remove the variability/flapping risk this behavior targets. I considered Workflow but rejected it because this is repeated evaluation of one model decision type, not a distinct multi-stage agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the configured short window against the real vision model path; assert decision consistency policy holds (no flip-flopping beyond allowed tolerance/window rule).
